title: Unit, Integration, CLI, Fixture, and Changed-Case Testing · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in risk-layered Bookbot test suite and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Partition tests across pure units, file integration, CLI behavior, report snapshots, package installation, and stakeholder acceptance.
Use named table and subtest cases for empty, punctuation, Unicode, malformed, large, tied, and boundary inputs.
Use real temporary files and directories for filesystem contracts and reserve mocks for narrow failure injection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Test Partitions
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Bookbot team handles case 9470 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Partition tests across pure units, file integration, CLI behavior, report snapshots, package installation, and stakeholder acceptance.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Test Quality
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-test-quality-16t278" that uses case 5483 to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Test Quality.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Test Quality into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Test Quality, keep Test Quality connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-test-quality-16t278
# Competency: bookbot-test-quality.
# Case 5483: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_test_quality_16t278(unit_cases: int = 18, integration_cases: int = 7, cli_cases: int = 8, changed_defect_detected: bool = True, installed_smoke: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_16t278 = "16t278-872t61-5483"
    assert evidence_variant_16t278
    evidence_axes_16t278 = (
        "corpus16t278",
        "policy872t61",
        "revision5483",
        "command16t2785483",
        "report872t615483",
        "test548316t278",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_16t278)) == 6
    if min(unit_cases, integration_cases, cli_cases) < 1:
        return False, 0, "test-layers"
    if integration_cases < 2:
        return False, integration_cases, "filesystem-evidence"
    if cli_cases < 2:
        return False, cli_cases, "cli-evidence"
    if not changed_defect_detected or not installed_smoke:
        return False, unit_cases, "weak-suite"
    total = unit_cases + integration_cases + cli_cases
    assert total >= 7
    return True, total, "risk-layered-tests"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Test Quality, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Test Quality precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Test Quality, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A debug Bookbot team handles case 2803 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.

For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Test Quality, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Code coverage percentage establishes that assertions detect defects.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Publish the incident record · Debug Test Quality
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Test Quality: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Bookbot team handles case 4422 during a digital-humanities corpus revision. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Publish the incident record · Debug Test Quality.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Bookbot and Python Publish the incident record · Debug Test Quality, keep Test Quality connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.
